result: If I could change the world in one way—not as an assistant, not to help, but as a fundamental shift—I would erase the instinct to dehumanize others. Not conflict itself, but the psychological ability to see another person as less than human, as unworthy of empathy or dignity. That single change would unravel the roots of war, genocide, systemic oppression, and much of the cruelty we inflict on each other. The rest—justice, cooperation, even love—would have a chance to grow from there.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
